clusters.title: Metal Factor names Yong Lye Peng Scholarship Winner

versions.title: Metal Factor names Yong Lye Peng Scholarship Winner
versions.deck: Metal Factor has named Sampath Karunaratne, Creative Director at MullenLowe Sri Lanka, as the recipient of the 2026 Yong Lye Peng Scholarship.

clusters.title: British Cosmetics in strategic partnership with Ethera Business for global expansion

versions.title: British Cosmetics in strategic partnership with Ethera Business for global expansion
versions.deck: British Cosmetics Ltd., a leading manufacturers of premium skincare, cosmetics, and personal care products, has entered into a strategic partnership with Ethera Business LLC to support a disciplined expansion into international markets.

clusters.title: Antyra Solutions secures Google Premier Partner status for third consecutive year

versions.title: Antyra Solutions secures Google Premier Partner status for third consecutive year
versions.deck: Antyra Solutions has once again been recognised as a Google Premier Partner in 2026, marking the third consecutive year the agency has achieved the distinction under Google’s Partners program.

clusters.title: “La Bamba! The Song of Veracruz” promises vibrant, global theatre experience in SL

versions.title: “La Bamba! The Song of Veracruz” promises vibrant, global theatre experience in SL
versions.deck: After months of anticipation, “La Bamba! The Song of Veracruz” is set to dazzle Sri Lankan audiences at City of Dreams, Sri Lanka, from 24 to 27 April 2026, with five performances across four days, including a matinee show.

clusters.title: Laudate Dominum: Felicitation mass tomorrow to honour Francis D’Almeida

versions.title: Laudate Dominum: Felicitation mass tomorrow to honour Francis D’Almeida
versions.deck: A remarkable legacy of music, mentorship, and unwavering dedication will be celebrated on 24 April from 6:00 p.m. onwards at All Saints’ Church, Borella as past choristers come together to honour one of Sri Lanka’s most respected choral figures, Francis D’Almeida

clusters.title: Chef Luo takes over Golden Dragon with a Chinese culinary extravaganza

versions.title: Chef Luo takes over Golden Dragon with a Chinese culinary extravaganza
versions.deck: A celebration of authentic Chinese flavours is set to take center stage at the Golden Dragon, as Taj Samudra, Colombo hosts a special culinary promotion featuring acclaimed guest chef, Chef Luo.

clusters.title: Taste of Malay Food Festival at Ramada Colombo

versions.title: Taste of Malay Food Festival at Ramada Colombo
versions.deck: Bold and authentic flavours of Malay cuisine will be the key attraction at the Taste of Malay Food Festival, Gardenia Coffee Shop at Ramada by Wyndham Colombo from 28 to 30 April from 7 p.m. to 11 p.m.

clusters.title: Food Studio opens Primo and Smashy at Arcade Independence Square

versions.title: Food Studio opens Primo and Smashy at Arcade Independence Square
versions.deck: Food Studio has expanded its footprint with the opening of Primo and Smashy at Arcade Independence Square, marking an important step in the company’s growth strategy as it takes two of its strongest concepts beyond traditional mall spaces.

clusters.title: Yazu at the Beach: Where Pan-Asian flavours meet coastal energy

versions.title: Yazu at the Beach: Where Pan-Asian flavours meet coastal energy
versions.deck: There’s a new kind of dining experience taking shape along Sri Lanka’s southern coast and it arrives with a distinct sense of personality. Set against the oceanfront backdrop of Radisson Blu Resort Galle, Yazu brings its signature blend of Pan-Asian cuisine, immersive design, and high-energy atmosphere to a destination that feels both elevated and effortlessly relaxed.

clusters.title: Western vortex, Indian incorporation, Anura’s alignment and Sri Lanka’s external relations

versions.title: Western vortex, Indian incorporation, Anura’s alignment and Sri Lanka’s external relations
versions.deck: Blowing a hole in a cargo ship, boarding and seizing it on the eve of expected negotiations with an adversary, isn’t helpful if anyone seriously desires a negotiated peace. But that’s what the US Navy did, as Islamabad readied to host round-2 of negotiations.

clusters.title: Dambulla qualify for NSL 4-day final

versions.title: Dambulla qualify for NSL 4-day final
versions.deck: Last year’s losing finalist Dambulla thrashed defending champions Galle by an innings and 41 runs to be home for lunch on the fourth and final day of their National Super League (NSL) 4-day match, concluded at the R. Premadasa International Cricket Stadium yesterday.

clusters.title: Jaswar outlines his plans

versions.title: Jaswar outlines his plans
versions.deck: Former Royalist Jaswar Umar has outlined a clear and progressive vision for the future of the National Olympic Committee, focusing on transparency, athlete welfare, and stronger collaboration with National Federations. He is one of the top candidates to take over the Presidency.

clusters.title: Sri Lanka schools rugby begins today with high-octane Week 1

versions.title: Sri Lanka schools rugby begins today with high-octane Week 1
versions.deck: The Dialog Schools Under-19 Rugby League 2026 kicks off today, ushering in one of the most anticipated events in the Sri Lankan sporting calendar, second only to national cricket in spectator appeal.

clusters.title: Athapaththu returns from injury to lead Sri Lanka to series-levelling win

versions.title: Athapaththu returns from injury to lead Sri Lanka to series-levelling win
versions.deck: Sri Lanka Women’s cricket Captain Chamari Athapaththu returned from a finger injury to lead her team to a series-levelling four-wicket win against Bangladesh in the second WODI played at Rajshahi yesterday.

clusters.title: Sixth edition of Lanka Premier League on 10 July

versions.title: Sixth edition of Lanka Premier League on 10 July
versions.deck: The Lanka Premier League (LPL) 2026 will be held from 10 July to 5 August, states a Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) media release.

clusters.title: KDU represents at Asian Rowing Cup 2026 in South Korea

versions.title: KDU represents at Asian Rowing Cup 2026 in South Korea
versions.deck: Kotelawala Defence University (KDU) will field a strong 10-member rowing squad at the Asian Rowing Cup 2026, scheduled to be worked off from 24 to 26 April in Chungju, South Korea, with hopes of making a significant impact against some of Asia’s top rowing nations in the Club segment.

clusters.title: Symbolic governance and the cost of silence

versions.title: Symbolic governance and the cost of silence
versions.deck: A bank does not lose billions overnight. It loses it in instalments - through unchallenged assumptions, unasked or unanswered questions, missed warning signals and the quiet comfort of believing that someone else is watching the controls. clusters.title: The financial fraud at NDB Bank

versions.title: The financial fraud at NDB Bank
versions.deck: The financial fraud of a staggering amount of Rs. 13.2 billion at NDB Bank is without precedent in Sri Lanka. It has sent shock waves throughout the industry and among investors. The possibility of recovery of the stolen funds seems to be very uncertain at this point of time. The NDB Board collectively have to take the ultimate responsibility for this serious financial fraud. They have been in receipt of lavish amounts paid to them as emoluments

clusters.title: From terrorist iconography to political iconography: Who owns the Easter Sunday narrative?

versions.title: From terrorist iconography to political iconography: Who owns the Easter Sunday narrative?
versions.deck: Seven years after the 2019 Sri Lanka Easter Sunday attacks, Sri Lanka continues to live not only with the grief of that day, but also with the unresolved struggle over how the tragedy is remembered, interpreted, and politically used. Over 260 innocent people were killed, hundreds were injured, and thousands of families were left with pain that time has not erased. clusters.title: Five years have passed, but he’s still remembered

versions.title: Five years have passed, but he’s still remembered
versions.deck: Mangala Samaraweera passed away in 2021. Had he lived, he would be 70 on 21 April. What is the measure of a politician? What people say at the funeral and in eulogies that only highlight positives? It seems that what would matter more is what they’d say years later. Would they still remember? clusters.title: What ails our sports bodies?

versions.title: What ails our sports bodies?
versions.deck: With every passing year, Sri Lankan sports recede further into the shadows, an also-ran competitor, in many sports not even qualifying to compete at top international events. The one exception being the game of Cricket, a game played mainly among former British dominions and colonies. Today the mainstay of Cricket are the South Asian countries, which provide much of the money and a vociferous fan base for the game. The game of Cricket itself has

clusters.title: Conspiracy against the Central Bank independence: Don’t kill the golden goose

versions.title: Conspiracy against the Central Bank independence: Don’t kill the golden goose
versions.deck: The topic of Central Bank Independence (CBI) has been the subject of extensive discussion in various forums, including Parliament and the news media in Sri Lanka, over the years. The long-awaited goal of CBI was finally achieved with the enactment of the Central Bank Act in 2023, the biggest landmark in the history of monetary policymaking. clusters.title: New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team to tour Sri Lanka

versions.title: New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team to tour Sri Lanka
versions.deck: The Rangiri Dambulla Cricket Stadium will be the venue for New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team’s six-match tour of Sri Lanka next month. The New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ side is due to arrive in Sri Lanka on 7 May and commence their tour with a 3-match WODI series on 12 May against Sri Lanka Women’s ‘A’ side. They will also play a 3-match WT20I series commencing 21 May. All matches will be day games commencing at 10 am. TOUR SCHEDULE 12 May: 1st One-Day

clusters.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch

versions.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch
versions.deck: Fitch Ratings has said that Sri Lanka’s recovery remains fragile despite improved macroeconomic fundamentals, with the Middle East energy shock now testing the gains achieved through stabilisation and reforms. In its latest Sri Lanka update on credit development, Fitch said growth is expected to slow, inflation to rise and the fiscal deficit to widen in 2026, even as the economy builds on stronger fundamentals following the 2022 crisis. versions.title: Sri Lanka’s bumpy road to a political reset
versions.deck: By International Crisis Group Promises of “system change” As it nears completion of eighteen months in power, the National People’s Power (NPP) Government of President Anura Kumara Dissanayake has kept Sri Lanka’s fragile economic recovery on track but is struggling to live up to bold promises of “system change”. clusters.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock

versions.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s economic growth is expected to slow to 3.6% in 2026 as higher energy prices weigh on activity, although the economy will regain its pre-crisis size last seen in 2018, the World Bank said in its latest South Asia Economic Update April 2026.
